title: Crepidiastrum surugense
intro: Crepidiastrum surugense, also known as Japanese Angelica, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. Research has shown that Crepidiastrum surugense possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and protect against cell damage. The plant's bioactive constituents include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Japanese medicine, Crepidiastrum surugense is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and relieve menstrual cramps.
taxonomy: Crepidiastrum surugense is classified in the family Tropaeolaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Crepidiastrum and is native to Japan. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the order Brassicales. The family Tropaeolaceae consists of annual or perennial plants and is commonly known as the nasturtium family. Crepidiastrum surugense is a small, herbaceous plant with delicate flowers.
